devices.name: Digital Monster (Original)
devices.notes: The foundation of the series with the standard 2-prong connector.

devices.name: Digimon Pendulum
devices.notes: Introduced the 'Shake' mechanic and Jogress fusion.

devices.name: Digimon Pendulum Cycle (Asia)
devices.notes: Bandai Asia exclusive; features accelerated aging and midnight resetting.

devices.name: Digimon Pendulum Progress
devices.notes: Advanced 2-prong raising with more complex evolution requirements.

devices.name: Digital Monster D-Cyber (Asia)
devices.notes: Flip-phone style shell; Ver. 1.5 includes unique Death-X evolutions like Death-X-DORUguremon.

devices.name: Digimon Twin
devices.notes: Utilizes 2-prong for legacy battles but primarily pairs via Infrared.

devices.name: Digital Monster Ver. 20th
devices.notes: Modern revival allowed raising two Digimon simultaneously.

devices.name: Digimon Pendulum Ver. 20th
devices.notes: Updated Pendulum with massive rosters and 2-prong link.

devices.name: Digimon Pendulum Z / Z II
devices.notes: Features 'Folder Continent' species and 2-prong connectivity.

devices.name: Digital Monster Original New Wave 1
devices.notes: Latest B&W revival released in March 2026 with new 'communication' features.

devices.name: Digimon Pendulum X
devices.notes: First series to use the 10-pin 3-prong connector and XAI system.

devices.name: Digimon Accel
devices.notes: High-spec 3-prong series with DNA sensor and removable faceplates.

devices.name: Digimon Mini
devices.notes: Ultra-compact shell utilizing the 3-prong 10-pin connector.

devices.name: Digital Monster X
devices.notes: Flagship modern 3-prong series with built-in quest maps.

devices.name: Digital Monster COLOR
devices.notes: First backlit color V-Pet with rechargeable battery and USB-C.

devices.name: Digimon Pendulum COLOR
devices.notes: Saiyu Warriors and Toho Braves released late 2025/early 2026.

devices.name: Digimon Color Collaboration
devices.notes: Special color firmware crossovers with custom evolution trees.

devices.name: Digital Monster COLOR XW
devices.notes: 15th Anniversary Xros Wars color remakes.

devices.name: Digivice (Original)
devices.notes: Pedometer-driven quest device for Digimon Adventure.

devices.name: D-3 (Digital Detect & Discover)
devices.notes: Adventure 02 tie-in with 2-prong connectivity.

devices.name: D-Ark (D-Power)
devices.notes: Uses a card-slash sensor and quest progression.

devices.name: D-Scanner (D-Tector)
devices.notes: Features a barcode scanner and 2-prong port.

devices.name: D-Spirit (Asia)
devices.notes: Asia exclusive handheld database/quest hybrid with 575+ Digimon.

devices.name: Digimon Neo (Asia)
devices.notes: Updated quest system based on the Digimon Next manga.

devices.name: Digivice 15th / Complete Selection
devices.notes: Enhanced B&W screen revivals.

devices.name: Digivice: (2020)
devices.notes: Adventure: Reboot series pedometer device.

devices.name: Digivice iC
devices.notes: Uses the 3-prong port to link with Pendulum X and Accel V-Pets.

devices.name: Digivice Burst
devices.notes: Upgraded iC hardware with Air Signal sensor and 3-prong port.

devices.name: Digimon Xros Loader
devices.notes: First color TFT device; supports MP3 playback and 'Digi-Melody'.

devices.name: Appli Drive / Appli Drive DUO
devices.notes: Utilizes Appmon chips to scan data into the color interface.

devices.name: Digivice -25th COLOR EVOLUTION-
devices.notes: Full color remake of the 1999 Adventure quest with anime visuals.

devices.name: D-3 -25th COLOR EVOLUTION-
devices.notes: Full color remake of the 02 quest line.

devices.name: D-Ark -25th COLOR EVOLUTION-
devices.notes: Latest 2026 release with physical card-slash color integration.
